channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: New Research Highlights Low PC Player Share for PlayStation Ports
item.content:encoded:
Recent sales data indicate that some PlayStation 5 games on PC are increasingly losing their audience share. This research follows the report that PlayStation is moving away from porting its first-party PlayStation 5 titles to PC.

Sony has been transferring several of its console exclusives to the PlayStation platform, including titles like Horizon Zero Dawn and the Spider-Man games. However, in early 2025, reports indicated that Sony plans to move away from this practice. Recent data from Newzoo (via GamesIndustry.Biz) shows that, on average, 13% of players for PS4 and PS5 titles are from the PC market when these games are ported.

“Newzoo’s data shows that PlayStation titles ported to PC after their console launch typically see PC account for around 13% of total players in the first three months across both releases,” says Manu Rosier, Director of Market Intelligence at Newzoo. “By comparison, when comparable AAA titles launch simultaneously on PC and console, PC contributes closer to 44% of players in the same period.”

Release Timing Significantly Impacts Players’ Choosing to Purchase PC Ports of PlayStation Games

It’s further noted that the choice to release games on PC much later, in some cases years later, than on PlayStation significantly impacts players’ decisions to pick up a game on PC. It’s noted that only 5% of players of Marvel’s Spider-Man 2 are on PC. That number is down from the 14% of the first Spider-Man release.

“The key takeaway is that release timing has a significant impact on PC engagement. When a PC version arrives years after the console launch, much of the early lifecycle demand has already been captured on the primary platform,” said Rosier.

Read more about the future of PlayStation and the potential release date of the PS6 at this link. In contrast, Xbox’s next project is known to be a PC-console hybrid. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about PlayStation games no longer coming to PC? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Slay the Spire 2 Devs Are ‘Microtransaction Haters’
item.content:encoded:
The developers of Slay the Spire hate microtransactions, and they want players to have the same experience.

Game developer and co-founder of Mega Crit, Casey Yano, is riding the wave of the mega success of Slay the Spire 2’s Early Access release. Recently, he was interviewed by Destructoid. In that interview, he makes it very clear that Mega Crit is not a fan of microtransactions, even though they have an audience that would very much support whatever they put out.

“We really want players to experience all of the same content as discussion of game content and balance is sort of our lifeblood,” he said.

Mega Crit may hate microtransactions, but they do support the modding community. In the interview, Yano notes that both Slay the Spire titles are easy to mod. However, the sequel is still in such an early stage that it’s not yet clear how much will be updated during this early testing phase.

“A lot of our focus this time around is reducing friction, so players have more resources and easier entry points to work with mods,” he said. “At the moment, I’m not entirely sure what we’ll update often throughout early access. Hopefully pure content. The good stuff.”

Fans can read Mega Crit’s comments on the massive success of Slay the Spire 2’s Early Access launch here.

Have you tried out Slay the Spire 2 yet? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Shigeru Miyamoto Once Compared Hunt for Game Secrets to Pornography
item.content:encoded:
Shigeru Miyamoto’s comments have been unearthed, where he compares gamers’ appetites for hidden secrets and video games to pornography. In the mid to late 1980s, no one was more synonymous with creating good video games than Nintendo and Shigeru Miyamoto.

An interview was conducted with Miyamoto back in 1989 and published in Gamer Handbook. That interview has recently been translated by shmupulations. In the interview, Miyamoto discusses how much more open the idea of a video game becomes when you place it under the umbrella of pure entertainment.

“You could say that a great movie or a great book is a ‘game,’ too… but they are passive, whereas the computer alone is interactive. So, within the wider world of entertainment, you have this specific category of interactive entertainment, and video games are just one part of that,” he said.

Further into the conversation, Miyamoto spoke about how pornography can sometimes elevate into the grotesque. He believed that the desire for secrets and games was reaching that level, saying that some of it wasn’t even measured by common sense.

“It’s just people getting bored and looking for stronger and stronger stimulation. We’ve hit a wall. When you’re at the point where you have no choice but to go ‘grotesque,’ you have to start thinking of new ways to use the medium,” he said.

Nintendo Continues to Innovate

Nintendo would find new ways to use the medium over the next 3 decades. From motion controls in the Nintendo Wii to the 2-in-1 gameplay of the Nintendo Switch, the developers continue to innovate. These days, Shigeru Miyamoto is more involved in expanding Nintendo’s core properties.

These expansions include movies and theme parks. The next major movie from Nintendo is Super Mario Galaxy. That will be released on April 1. The final trailer for the movie will be revealed on March 9 in a special Nintendo Direct presentation. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about Miyamoto’s quotes from 1989? Do you think they’ve only continued to ring true as the years have gone on? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Mega Crit Comments on Early Access Success of Slay the Spire 2
item.content:encoded:
Mega Crit is “blown away” by Slay the Spire 2’s Early Access launch after the long-awaited sequel to the tentpole deckbuilder soared to the top of the SteamDB player charts.

Slay the Spire 2 entered Early Access on Steam on March 5. In that time, the game has had a very positive launch. Peak player count rose over 500,000 in less than 3 days. Now, Mega Crit has expressed its gratitude to players for the game’s success so far.

Slay the Spire 2 Dev They’ll Eventually Catch Hollow Knight: Silksong

In a tweet, the Mega Crit team said they were taken aback by the game’s success and noted that they’re now even more driven to make Slay the Spire 2 the best game it can be. They also joked that they would eventually catch up to Hollow Knight: Silksong’s success.

“Our team is TOTALLY blown away by the amount of people who have been playing & sharing their love for the game we’ve been working on for the past half-decade.

We’re excited to continue to make StS2 the best that it can be!! 💪

Also obligatory joke: we’ll getcha one day, Silksong”

Learn more about Slay the Spire 2 at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest on the game as Early Access marches on.

Have you played the game yet? What do you think about the deckbuilder thus far? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: WWE 2K26 Patch 1.03 Addresses Stability Issues and More
item.content:encoded:
WWE 2K26‘s latest patch is live. Here are the full details of the problems that the latest patch addresses.

Visual Concepts has released the patch notes for WWE 2K26 Patch 1.03. The patch addresses overall stability issues and specific issues related to certain modes. Of note, while the patch is live for PlayStation, PC, and Xbox users, it will arrive on Nintendo Switch 2 later in the week.

WWE 2K26 Patch 1.03 Full Notes

General
– Stability and Performance improvements

Gameplay
– Addressed reported concerns over match restarts not working as intended during 3 Stages of Hell matches

Create
– Addressed reported concerns of images not mapping correctly

The Island
– Addressed reported concerns of leaderboard instability

More on WWE 2K26

WWE 2K26 is now available for those who pre-ordered the premium editions of the game. The standard edition will be released globally on Friday, March 13.

Several updates are planned for the game, including the addition of 24 new wrestlers through the newly implemented Ringside Pass system. You can find the complete list of DLC wrestlers here.

Learn more about the Ringside Pass system at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest updates on the latest WWE 2K title as they become available.

If you’re already playing it, are you enjoying WWE 2K26 so far? Have you encountered any of the bugs mentioned above? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Today’s Strands #735 Hints and Answers for March 8, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #735 for Sunday, March 8, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 8, 2026

The Strands’ answers for Game #735 are all about the change in time that occurred in the United States of America. So Spring Forward and think about Daylight Savings.

General Strands Hints For March 8

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Nothing’s better than a good night’s rest. What’s another word for rest?
  • Hint 2: 60 minutes.
  • Hint 3: Opposite of reverse.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #735 for March 8, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 4, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • RING
  • RINGS
  • SING
  • DRAW
  • LOCK

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #735 On March 8, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • SPRING
  • HOUR
  • SLEEP
  • FORWARD
  • LOSING
  • CLOCK
  • SPANAGRAM: DAYLIGHTSAVINGS

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #735?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 9 will be Strands Game #736.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/todays-strands-735-hints-and-answers-for-march-8-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1002)—Answers for Monday, March 10
item.content:encoded:
It’s a whole new week, and that means it’s time for a new round of NYT Connections hints and solutions. Did you manage to scoop the 1000th NYT Connections badge last week? Let me know in the comments below. In the meantime, just scroll down to get today’s NYT Connections hints and answers in just a few seconds.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (March 9)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • WAREHOUSE
  • FISHBOWL
  • WEARABLE
  • HOT SEAT
  • WEREWOLF
  • MICROSCOPE
  • WHEREFORE
  • SPOTLIGHT
  • ANIMAL
  • COMPANY
  • BEAKER
  • E STREET BAND
  • FOZZIE
  • MAFIA
  • GONZO
  • VIDEO GAME

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words that start with the same thing spelled differently
  • Green – Things you might associate with being scrutinized
  • Blue – Popular Muppets characters
  • Purple – Things that feature a ‘boss’ character

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, March 9:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, March 9 (#1002)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – WAREHOUSE, WEARABLE, WEREWOLF, WHEREFORE
  • Green – FISHBOWL, HOT SEAT, MICROSCOPE, SPOTLIGHT
  • Blue – ANIMAL, BEAKER, FOZZIE, GONZO
  • Purple – COMPANY, E STREET BAND, MAFIA, VIDEO GAME

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP
